items.headline: Council reviews major capital projects and spending for next fiscal year
items.summary: The City Council held a study session on April 14 to discuss the Fiscal Year 2026-27 Capital Improvement Program, which outlines significant infrastructure and development projects Mountain View plans to fund. This is worth tracking if you care about what physical changes and improvements are coming to the city.

items.headline: Sunnyvale updates tenant protection rules in new ordinance
items.summary: The Housing and Human Services Commission introduced changes to Chapter 19.71 of the Municipal Code regarding residential tenant protections. This ordinance amendment could affect how tenant rights are enforced in the city.

items.headline: City releases Library Facilities and Customer Experience Plan
items.summary: San José presented a status report on its Library Facilities and Customer Experience Plan on April 9, which outlines improvements to library services and physical spaces across the city. This plan affects where residents can access library services and what amenities will be available.

items.headline: Downtown historic building seeks major expansion bonus
items.summary: The property at 405 Kipling Street is requesting elevation from a Category 4 to Category 2 historic resource designation, which would allow an additional 5,000 square feet of building area as a bonus for historic and seismic rehabilitation. This zoning decision will determine how much the building can be expanded while preserving its historic character.

items.headline: Major mixed-use development proposed on San Antonio Road
items.summary: A developer is proposing to rezone and redevelop two properties into an 8-story building with 167 apartments (28 affordable units) and 1,400 sq ft of commercial space on San Antonio Road. This significant upzoning and density increase will go before the City Council for review.

items.headline: 333 Franklin Street zoning hearing postponed indefinitely
items.summary: A zoning hearing for a project at 333 Franklin Street was cancelled on April 8 with no new date set. The project will need to be re-noticed once city staff schedules a new hearing, suggesting potential issues or delays with the development proposal.

items.headline: City approves construction contract with QLM, Inc.
items.summary: The Town Manager was authorized to execute a construction contract with QLM, Inc., though the agenda item details were incomplete. Residents should watch for follow-up information about what project this contract covers and its impact on the community.

items.headline: Bascom Avenue Highway-Rail Traffic Signal Project moves forward
items.summary: City Council approved a third amendment to the consultant agreement for the Bascom Avenue Highway-Rail Traffic Signal Project on April 7. This ongoing project addresses traffic and safety concerns at the intersection of Bascom Avenue and railroad crossings.
